calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-15-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Brewere for and with Bridgette Walker.

Ms. Brewer stated that Mr. Malone called her and informed her that he could not appear today and that he was submitting on his pleadings. In addition, Ms. Brewer stated that she did not know if the Franchise Tax Board had been paid, that she had set aside a reserve of $20,000.00 since she is not sure what will happen with the claim, that there are outstanding taxes that have not been paid which is approxiamately $1,500.00 plus $900.00 in fees, that the tax preparer will not release the taxes until paid, and that the money is in a blocked account and they cannot get to it. Ms. Brewer went on to address the extraordinary fees stating that the personal representative asked for money for cleaning up the premises, that there were vehicles/boats to be removed, that there was solvent on the premises, that it was a double lot, and that it was "knee deep" in junk to clean up. The Court stated that someone was paid $15,000.00 to clean it up. Ms. Brewer stated that Ms. Walker supervised the clean up, that she cut trees, and moved pieces of cement. Ms. Walker stated that four large dumpsters were filled, that there was an issue with rodents, and that she was hands on with the clean up. Ms. Brewer stated as to the Petition for Entitlement that they appeared at hearings, that they knew the Father of the decedent was Murphy Jones not Mr. White, that they provided a birth certificate, and that once they identified the lawful heir they stopped work and only monitored the matter. Ms. Brewer addressed the extraordinary fees stating that they had appeared on a couple of attempts to sell the property which were unsuccessful and that Ms. Walker did not have independent authority so they would have to come to court for approval which consumed a lot of hours. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Aguerre.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Brewer for and with Bridgette Hendricks.

Ms. Brewer stated that some of the items in the Examiner's notes have been cleared up but not all of the receipts have not been located. The Court stated that if the presonal representative cannot account there will be a surcharge. Bridgette Hendricks addressed the Court regarding the missing receipts. At the request of counsel the matter was continued to February 2, 2009.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-27-2008 Continued To Nov-24-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Brewer for and with Bridgette Walker (Petitioner).

The Court stated that there were multiple issues with the accounting petition. Ms. Brewer stated that Ms. Walker was gathering more information, requested permission to abandon the automobile stating that it was inoperable, that it was about to be towed, and that there was no interest on behalf of any of the beneficiaries. The Court granted her request as to the abandonment of the vehicle (subject to a written order). Ms. Brewer was directed to file an amended account/petition addressing the remaining issues (gas expenses, payment to Bryan Taylor, $7,700.00 in counter checks, statutory fees, balancing of the account). The matter was continued to November 24, 2008 for status on the filing of the amended account.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Hill.

Off Calendar Mar-21-2007 Continued To Apr-04-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Brewer (Counsel for Bridgette Walker, Objector).

Ms. Brewer reported on the status of the real property sale, noting that the sale was close to completion. She also reported that all parties had reached agreement as to the distribution scheme scheme for the estate. Consequently, the Court granted the Petition to Determine Entitlement on the terms agreed to by the parties (Ms. Brewer to prepare the Order) and continued the status hearing on the receipts for the blocked account to April 4, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.
